hypothesis: Training a diffusion-based crystal generator (e.g., DiffCSP) with an auxiliary loss that penalizes a neural surrogate of energy-above-hull trained on ≤5% of static-DFT-labeled OQMD compositions will at least double the fraction of generated structures with energy-above-hull ≤0.1 eV/atom (SUN stability) among 1,024 samples relative to the same architecture trained without this loss under an identical DFT evaluation setup.
